'''Paul Whatuira''' (pronounced {{IPAc-en|f|æ|t|uː|w|ɪər|ə}}, born 31 July 1981) is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] in the NRL in 2011. A New Zealand international {{rlp|ce}}, he won [[National Rugby League]] premierships with the [[Penrith Panthers]] and [[Wests Tigers]] and achieved success with the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the Super League.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Whatuira was born in [[Wellington, New Zealand]].

==Playing career==
===Early years===
Whatuira played his junior rugby league at [[Wainuiomata Lions|Wainuiomata]], near Wellington, where he played with former Huddersfield teammate [[David Faiumu]].<ref>[http://www.accessmylibrary.com/article-1G1-136690148/wainui-boys-take-centre.html Wainui Boys to take Centre stage in Fairytale Grand Final] ''NZPA'', 26 September 2005</ref> He also played [[rugby union]] as a schoolboy.<ref name=union /> He followed the [[Canberra Raiders]] as a child and idolised [[Mal Meninga]].

He represented [[Wellington rugby league team|Wellington]] in 1999.<ref>[http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-17376838.html Young star hurts Canty] {{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''The Press'', 29 March 1999</ref>

===Auckland Warriors===
Whatuira left Wellington at 17 to join the [[Auckland Warriors]].<ref>Peter Bidwell [http://www.accessmylibrary.com/coms2/summary_0286-23461328_ITM Whatuira happy beavering away] ''The Dominion Post'', 31 May 2003</ref>
He made his first grade début for Auckland Warriors against Wests Tigers in the [[Leichhardt Oval]] on 14 February 2000, in Round 2. He played in four other first grade games for the Warriors that season, before leaving the club at the end of the year. During the 2000 season he was occasionally released to the [[Wainuiomata Lions]] and played in the [[Bartercard Cup]].<ref>John Coffey [http://www.encyclopedia.com/doc/1P2-17472968.html Whatuira boosts Wainui backline] ''The Press'', 8 April 2000</ref>

===Melbourne Storm===
He spent 2001 at the [[Melbourne Storm]], playing in six first grade games.
[[File:Paul Whateriera (16 April 2005).jpg|thumb|left|Whatuira in 2004]]

===Penrith Panthers===
Whatuira joined the [[Penrith Panthers]] in 2002 and became a first grade regular. He played left centre in the Panthers side that won the [[2003 NRL grand final]]. As 2003 NRL premiers, the Panthers travelled to England to face [[Super League VIII]] champions, the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2004 World Club Challenge]]. Whatuira played at centre in the Panthers' 22–4 loss. His 2003 [[premiership ring]] was stolen from his home along with his DVD player in 2005. Two years later the police recovered the ring when they raided a suspected criminal's home.<ref>[http://www.theage.com.au/news/NATIONAL/Police-find-stolen-NRL-premiership-ring/2007/11/14/1194766737161.html Police find stolen NRL premiership ring] ''The Age'', 14 November 2007</ref>

===Wests Tigers===
Whatuira joined the Wests Tigers in 2005 and played at centre in the Tigers' [[2005 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], gaining his second premiership ring.
As NRL Premiers Wests faced [[Super League]] champions [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Whatuira played at centre in the Tigers' 30–10 loss.
Whatuira had a forgettable 2006, he was sin-binned in the Tigers' loss to the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in [[Dairy Farmers Stadium|Townsville]] just before half-time with [[Matt Sing]] scoring in his absence. In the return match, he suffered a [[hamstring]] injury which was aggravated during the warm-up versus [[Brisbane Broncos]] meaning he would not play for the rest of the campaign.

===Huddersfield Giants===
After considering a switch to [[rugby union]] with the [[New South Wales Waratahs]],<ref name=union>{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/nrl/news/article.cfm?c_id=270&objectid=10440202|title=League: Whatuira considers code switch|author=Geenty, Mark|date=17 May 2007|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=2 November 2011}}</ref> Whatuira instead moved to England and joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 2008 where he is contracted until the end of the 2010 season.<ref>[https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/6936798.stm Giants sign Kiwi centre Whatuira] ''[[BBC Sport]]'', 8 August 2007</ref> Whatuira played in the Giants [[2009 Challenge Cup]] Final appearance, where they lost to the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/oct/14/paul-whatuira-huddersfield-giants Huddersfield Giants' Paul Whatuira in police custody] ''The Guardian'', 14 October 2009</ref>

===Parramatta Eels===
On 21 December 2010, Whatuira signed a one-year deal with the Parramatta Eels for the 2011 season. Whatuira is expected to fill the void left in the backline by departing senior players of [[Eric Grothe Jr]] and [[Timana Tahu]]. Eels CEO [[Paul Osborne]] stated that "Paul Whatuira is a proved international performer who brings class and experience to our backline".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/whatuira-signs-eels-3981059|title=Whatuira signs Eels|website=tvnz.co.nz|access-date=3 June 2023|archive-date=3 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230603094727/https://www.tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/whatuira-signs-eels-3981059|url-status=dead}}</ref> However, Whatuira retired after just appearing in one game for the Eels.<ref>{{cite news|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Walker's off: veteran hangs up the boots|author=Glenn Jackson |url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/walkers-off-veteran-hangs-up-the-boots-20110729-1i45b.html|access-date=30 July 2011|date=30 July 2011}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Whatuira represented the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] on ten occasions between 2004 and 2006, including the 24–0 win over [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] at [[Elland Road]] in the [[2005 Rugby League Tri Nations|2005 Tri Nations tournament]], in which he scored a try. Whatuira was also selected in the 19-man squad for the one-off New Zealand vs Australia Centenary test in [[Wellington, New Zealand]] on 14 October 2007.

Whatuira is also eligible for the Cook Islands and was named in their train on squad for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].<ref>[http://dazrl.awardspace.info/2000_wc.htm 2000 World Cup] ''Darryl Bradford's Rugby League''</ref> He instead represented [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|Aotearoa Māori]] at the tournament.

==Charity work==
During 2006, Whatuira posed for the ''[[Naked Rugby League 2007-08]]'' charity calendar, which was produced to raise money for the National Breast Cancer Foundation of Australia.

Paul also posed alongside former Wests Tigers teammates [[Robbie Farah]] and [[Dene Halatau]] for the ''Naked for a Cause 2008-09'' calendar for the [[McGrath Foundation]] charity.

==Arrest==
On 13 October 2009, Whatuira was arrested and underwent a psychiatric assessment after he allegedly assaulted two men.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/2966121/Ex-Kiwi-league-rep-detained-in-double-assault-inquiry|title=Ex-Kiwi league rep detained in double assault inquiry|date=15 October 2009|work=stuff.co.nz|access-date=2009-10-15}}</ref> Police used a [[Taser]] gun to subdue him.<ref>Gareth Walker [https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/oct/15/paul-whatuira-huddersfield-giants-mental-hospital Huddersfield's Paul Whatuira assessed in mental health hospital after arrest] ''The Guardian'', 15 October 2009</ref> Whatuira spent 13 days in a secure psychiatric hospital after the incident which was reportedly related to sexual abuse he suffered as a child.<ref name="Stuff.co.nz_3235468">{{cite news|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/3235468/Whatuira-I-wanted-to-kill-myself|title=Paul Whatuira: I wanted to kill myself|author=Reid, Neil|date=17 January 2010|work=[[Sunday News (New Zealand)|Sunday News]]|access-date=2 November 2011}}</ref><ref>James Phelps [http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/day-i-nearly-killed-myself-paul-whatuira/story-e6frexnr-1225837967753 Day I nearly killed myself: Paul Whatuira] ''Daily Telegraph'', 8 March 2010</ref> Whatuira was not charged over the incident as under section two of the British Crimes Act, he is exempt from potential prosecution as he was deemed "mentally ill".<ref name="TVNZ_3331109">{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/whatuira-being-arrested-saved-my-life-3331109|title=Whatuira: Being arrested saved my life|date=17 January 2010|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=2 November 2011}}</ref>

== Match Fit and personal life ==
In 2024, he made his debut with [[Carlos Spencer]] in ''Match Fit: Union vs. League''. Whatuira also beat Spencer in a charity Fight For Life boxing match. He also came first on the initial bronco endurance test at 5:37, two seconds faster than [[Louis Anderson]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 1 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-1/S2339-580/M87456-547 |access-date=2024-07-29 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref> He improved to 5:17 on the final test, while Luis improved to 5:32.<ref name=":0" /> In 2023, he co-founded The Foundry Boxing Club.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 5 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-5/S2339-580/M88774-671 |access-date=2024-08-24 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref> He is also a motivational speaker, founder and director of Inner Strength.

Mentally: He revealed that he was sexually abused as a child, suffered from [[psychosis]], paranoia and had come close to committing suicide. He is engaged to Trina Tamati as of 2024, and had a daughter, Gabrielle from a previous marriage, while Trina had a daughter from a previous marriage, while they also have a 3-year-old son.<ref name=":0" />

Despite already in a good shape, he managed to get a perfect -15 metabolic age in the final weigh-in.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Fit Season 4 Ep 6 {{!}} SPORT {{!}} ThreeNow |url=https://www.threenow.co.nz/shows/match-fit/season-4-ep-6/S2339-580/M89103-687 |access-date=2024-08-28 |website=www.threenow.co.nz}}</ref>
